findings.title: ERC1155 mint callback reentrancy lets contract stakers mint the same pending points repeatedly

findings.title: Pool accounting becomes insolvent with fee-on-transfer or balance-decreasing stake tokens

best_agent_match.title: Pool accounting is insolvent for fee-on-transfer or balance-mutating stake tokens

findings.title: Reward parameter changes retroactively rewrite past emissions for untouched pools

best_agent_match.title: Missing Event Emission for Shop Setting

rejected_candidates.title: Reward Theft via Emergency Withdraw Without Pool Update
rejected_candidates.reason: `emergencyWithdraw()` zeroes both `user.amount` and `user.rewardDebt` before returning funds. The caller has no stake left to claim from any later `accPointsPerShare` change, so this does not let them steal other users' rewards.

rejected_candidates.title: Uninitialized Shop Variable Causes Contract Lock
rejected_candidates.reason: `shop` being unset at deployment is an initialization requirement, not a permanent lock vulnerability. The owner can set it later through `setShop()`, so this is at most an operational/configuration concern.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Pool ID Bounds Check
rejected_candidates.reason: Dynamic array indexing already reverts on out-of-bounds access in Solidity. That is the standard safeguard here, and the claim about reading stale storage past array bounds is incorrect.

rejected_candidates.title: Precision Loss in Reward Distribution
rejected_candidates.reason: Integer truncation dust is expected in share-based reward accounting and no concrete exploit or material protocol harm was shown from these divisions.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Event Emission for Shop Setting
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an informational observability issue, not a reportable vulnerability under the requested impact bar.
